They know the temple complex inside out, understand crowd patterns, and follow ethical  ...    Unlicensed guides offer cheaper rates but lack proper training and often push commission-based detours that waste your  ...    ### Common Angkor Wat Guide Scams And How to Avoid Them

Unlicensed guides at temple entrances Unlicensed guides approach tourists outside major temples offering cheap rates and immediate service. They lack official badges, provide incorrect historical information, and often lead you to commission-based shops disguised as cultural stops. title: Angkor Wat for First-Time Visitors: Avoid Common Mistakes
highlights:
comprehensive guide reveals the 15 most critical errors first-timers make at Cambodia’s UNESCO World Heritage  ...    Your Angkor Pass includes a photo taken at purchase, so arrive prepared for this requirement. The photo prevents sharing passes between people and ensures proper identification at temple checkpoints throughout the archaeological park. Guard your pass carefully—replacement passes aren’t issued for lost or damaged tickets. Keep it accessible for frequent checkpoint inspections but protected from Cambodia’s humidity and rain that can damage the photo and barcode needed for entry validation. If you purchase your pass after 5:00 PM, it becomes valid the following day, effectively giving you bonus time if you  ...    ## Mistake #3: Inappropriate Clothing Choices

### The Dress Code Reality Inappropriate clothing causes more temple access denials than any other single factor, with thousands of visitors turned away daily for exposed shoulders, short skirts, or revealing tops

title: Tourism Info - APSARA National Authority
highlights: APSARA National Authority has been responsible for the conservation and sustainable development of Angkor since 1995. One of its goals is to harmonize tourist experiences with public safety and respect towards the community. This official Visitor Code of Conduct was designed to support this goal.
